questions.question: Which situation best represents the WHO definition of health?

options.C: A person physically fit but socially isolated
options.D: A person physically healthy, mentally stable, and socially active

questions.explanation: WHO defines health as complete physical, mental, and social well-being.

questions.question: Which example best shows the relationship between environment and health?

options.B: Living in a polluted area leading to respiratory illness

questions.explanation: Environmental pollution can directly affect human health.

questions.question: Which of the following combinations correctly matches disease and type of pathogen?
questions.explanation: Tuberculosis is caused by the bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

questions.question: Which statement correctly distinguishes symptoms from signs?

options.A: Symptoms are measurable and signs are felt
options.B: Symptoms are experienced by patients while signs are observable by doctors

questions.explanation: Symptoms are felt by the patient; signs are measurable or observable indicators.

questions.question: Which factor mainly contributes to the spread of communicable diseases in communities?
questions.explanation: Poor sanitation and overcrowding facilitate disease transmission.

questions.question: Which pair correctly represents vector and disease?
questions.explanation: Dengue virus is transmitted by Aedes mosquitoes.

questions.question: Which scenario best explains non-communicable diseases?

options.C: Diseases caused by genetic or lifestyle factors

questions.explanation: Non-communicable diseases often arise from genetics or lifestyle habits.

questions.question: Which of the following actions most effectively prevents vector-borne diseases?
questions.explanation: Mosquitoes breed in stagnant water; removing it prevents vector reproduction.

questions.question: Which situation demonstrates antibiotic resistance?

options.B: Bacteria surviving after incomplete antibiotic treatment

questions.explanation: Incomplete antibiotic use allows bacteria to survive and develop resistance.

questions.question: Which condition increases the risk of lifestyle diseases?

options.C: Sedentary lifestyle and unhealthy eating habits

questions.explanation: Poor diet and lack of physical activity contribute to lifestyle diseases.

questions.question: Which statement correctly explains immunity?

options.A: The body’s ability to digest food
options.B: The body’s defense system against pathogens
options.D: The ability to maintain body temperature

questions.explanation: Immunity protects the body from harmful microorganisms.

questions.question: Which example shows active immunity?
questions.explanation: Vaccines stimulate the immune system to produce its own antibodies.

questions.question: Which scientist is associated with the discovery of antibiotics?
questions.explanation: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin.

questions.question: Which example best illustrates social well-being as part of health?
questions.explanation: Social well-being refers to healthy relationships and social interactions.

questions.question: Which disease is caused by protozoa and transmitted by a vector?
questions.explanation: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium protozoa and spread by mosquitoes.

questions.question: Which action contributes most to public health improvement?
questions.explanation: Vaccination programs protect entire populations.

questions.question: Which factor primarily determines the spread of water-borne diseases?
questions.explanation: Contaminated water spreads diseases like cholera and typhoid.

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes herd immunity?

options.B: Vaccination protects individuals and reduces disease spread in the community

questions.explanation: Herd immunity protects communities when many individuals are vaccinated.

questions.question: Which factor mainly influences mental health?
questions.explanation: Mental health is influenced by genetics, environment, and stress.

questions.question: Which practice most effectively strengthens immunity naturally?

options.A: Balanced diet, exercise, and proper sleep

questions.explanation: Healthy lifestyle habits improve immune function.

questions.question: Which statement best describes communicable diseases?

options.B: They spread through pathogens from one host to another

questions.explanation: Communicable diseases spread via pathogens.

questions.question: Which factor increases vulnerability to infectious diseases?
questions.explanation: Malnutrition weakens the immune system.

questions.question: Which combination correctly matches disease and transmission route?
questions.explanation: Malaria spreads through mosquito bites.

questions.question: Which global organization monitors disease outbreaks and public health policies?
questions.explanation: WHO coordinates global health policies and disease monitoring.

questions.question: Which strategy is most effective in preventing the spread of infectious diseases in a population?
questions.explanation: Vaccination and sanitation are key public health measures.
